summary.intent: Add GitHub Issues and Discussions links with icons to the web UI top bar, then display live open counts fetched from the GitHub API with local caching.
summary.outcome: Both links with GitHub Octicon SVG icons were added, a github-stats.ts module was created to fetch and cache counts in localStorage with 1-hour TTL, and badge spans display the counts inline (always visible, empty on error). All changes were committed on a feature branch.

repo: Icon masks are SVG files stored in crates/web/src/assets/icons/masks/ and referenced via CSS mask-image in crates/web/ui/input.css; the compiled style.css resolves paths relative to crates/web/src/assets/.
The Tailwind build pipeline: input.css in crates/web/ui/ → compiled to crates/web/src/assets/css/style.css via build.sh.
JS modules are bundled with Vite from crates/web/ui/src/; new modules are imported in app.tsx to be included in the bundle.
Nav count badges use .nav-count class in layout.css with .visible toggling display; a new .header-badge class was added for header counts.
GitHub Discussions count is NOT available via REST API for public repos without auth — only via GraphQL API. Issues count is available unauthenticated via GitHub Search API.

code.finding: New module: fetches issues via /search/issues and discussions count (may 404 without auth), caches both in localStorage under 'moltis-github-stats' with 1-hour TTL, applies cached values immediately then refreshes stale data.
.header-badge is always display:inline-block (not hidden on error); only the text content is empty when count is unavailable.
New modules are wired in by importing at module load time; no explicit init call needed.

workflow: Compiled CSS asset paths resolve relative to the output file location, not the source input.css location — important when referencing asset URLs in CSS.
Biome is used for linting TS files before committing.
Type checking with tsc and linting with biome are run before the Vite build as part of the pre-commit workflow.

friction: Icon CSS path resolution required investigation — input.css is in ui/ but mask URLs resolve relative to the compiled style.css output in src/assets/, causing initial confusion about where to place SVG files.
GitHub Discussions count is unavailable via unauthenticated REST API; a workaround using pagination header parsing was attempted but may silently fail (404/403) in production.
Multiple redundant tool calls (repeated Grep for navCountSkills/navCountCrons, repeated reads of app.tsx) suggest some uncertainty about the codebase structure.
open_items: Discussions count fetch may return null silently if the REST endpoint requires auth — the GraphQL API with a stored PAT would be the proper fix but was deferred.
Issues count via Search API includes PRs in the count — a more accurate query filtering to type:issue only may be needed.

No backend proxy/caching was implemented; the 60 req/hour unauthenticated rate limit could be a problem if many users load the page simultaneously from different IPs (each client has its own limit, but shared IP scenarios like corporate NAT could hit it).
